turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: The dinner in Spanish is la cena, spelt c-e-n-a, la cena.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: La cena. Those in Spain will pronounce /la thena/. So la cena, the dinner. Now, to dine, which doesn’t sound formal in Spanish as it does in English, to dine, you just make la cena into a verb. So how do you think that would sound?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Cenar. Good. So cenar is to dine. So how would you say I was dining, or more naturally in English, I was having dinner?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Cenaba. Now, apart from I was having dinner, what else can that mean? Cenaba?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: And? Who else?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. So we have all of those meanings there with the - aba. So for this line in the past we have - aba for - ar verbs. We are taking off our - ar and we're putting - aba as our key sound, and then we are adding on more, so n or s. Quemar, q-u-e-m-a-r, quemar in Spanish is to burn.

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Quemar. Good. How would you say He was burning the dinner? He was burning the dinner:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Quemaba la cena. And if you wanted to show that it was he that was burning the dinner, how would you do that?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. And if you wanted to show that it was you (formal), You were burning the dinner:

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Usted quemaba la cena. Good. So Él quemaba la cena, he was burning, and then we say what he was burning: the dinner. If we want to say The dinner was burning, The dinner was getting, we can’t just say burnt La cena quemaba, because we are waiting to hear what was the dinner burning? It feels incomplete. So if you want to just say The dinner was burning, like The dinner was getting burnt, in Spanish we are going to say The dinner was burning itself. What is itself?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Se. So how would you say The dinner was burning, The dinner was getting burnt, The dinner was burning itself?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Very good. That’s it! La cena se quemaba. Good. The position of la cena is also flexible. You can say La cena se quemaba or you can put la cena afterwards. How would that be?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Se quemaba la cena. So it’s flexible, unlike in English, because in English, well we don’t really change verbs so much. There are not so many grammatical resources in English as there are in Spanish, for example, so in English the word order is much more important than in Spanish to show what’s going on. So we can say La cena se quemaba or Se quemaba la cena. Because we have se, we understand that the dinner was getting burnt, there was nobody else there involved. If, you’re also going to you want to say I burn, like in the sun, in the sense of, you know, I get burnt use this structure, no? You’re going to say. turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. Me quemo en el sol. You can’t just say Quemo en el sol, because this would mean that I burn something in the sun, and we will be waiting to hear what you burn in the sun. Me quemo en el sol. If you want to say The sun burns me, this is another way you could put the sentence, and there we are not using this “self” structure, because we have The sun burns and then we have me telling us what it burns, so it’s complete. So how would you say that, the sun burns me?

turns.speaker: tutor
turns.text: Good. El sol me quema. Me quema el sol. Either way. Another example of a verb like this is romper. Romper. Romper means – maybe you can guess it. What do you think romper means? It’s quite obscure, the connection, but you might find it. Rupture?

Todo, t-o-d-o, is all. So It breaks all the time, It breaks itself, it gets broken, all the time. How would you say that? It breaks all the time:
